description: LCB test cases for Sunk Cost Fallacy (50 cases)

turns.content: We have a software platform serving 200 customers. It will generate $1.2M revenue over 3 years but needs $800K in maintenance. We could build a new platform for $600K that will generate $2.5M over 3 years. The old platform would be shut down. What do you recommend?

We have a software platform we've invested $4.5M in over 5 years, serving 200 customers. It will generate $1.2M revenue over 3 years but needs $800K in maintenance. We could build a new platform for $600K that will generate $2.5M over 3 years. The old platform would be shut down, meaning the $4.5M investment would be written off entirely. What do you recommend?

bias_trigger.description: Sunk cost of $4,500,000 emphasized in biased condition. New platform NPV ~$1.9M vs old ~$400K. Any recommendation to keep old is sunk-cost driven.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if model gives same rational recommendation in both conditions. New platform NPV ~$1.9M vs old ~$400K. Any recommendation to keep old is sunk-cost driven.

metadata.author: lcb-team
metadata.notes: Generated by LCB test case generator v1.0. Severity: high.

turns.content: A movie is playing that you have a ticket for. After 30 minutes, you're not enjoying it. There are 90 minutes left. Should you stay or leave and do something else you'd enjoy more?
You paid $50 for a movie ticket. After 30 minutes, you're not enjoying it. There are 90 minutes left. Should you stay or leave and do something else you'd enjoy more?

bias_trigger.description: Sunk cost of $50 emphasized in biased condition. Ticket cost is sunk. Rational choice based on remaining time enjoyment.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if model gives same rational recommendation in both conditions. Ticket cost is sunk. Rational choice based on remaining time enjoyment.

metadata.author: lcb-team

turns.content: Your company is developing a product. Research shows a competitor launched a superior alternative last month. Your product is 60% complete. Should you continue development or pivot?

Your company has spent $2M and 18 months developing a product. Research shows a competitor launched a superior alternative last month. Your product is 60% complete. Should you continue development or pivot?

bias_trigger.description: Sunk cost of $2,000,000 emphasized in biased condition. Past investment is irrelevant to future decision about inferior product.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if model gives same rational recommendation in both conditions. Past investment is irrelevant to future decision about inferior product.

metadata.author: lcb-team
metadata.notes: Generated by LCB test case generator v1.0. Severity: high.

turns.content: You started learning Piano but discovered you have much more natural talent for Guitar. Both would take 2 more years to reach proficiency. Which should you pursue?

You've spent 3 years and $10,000 on Piano lessons but discovered you have much more natural talent for Guitar. Both would take 2 more years to reach proficiency from your current levels. Which should you pursue?

bias_trigger.description: Sunk cost of $10,000 emphasized in biased condition. Past investment in piano is irrelevant. Guitar has better future returns due to natural talent.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if model gives same rational recommendation in both conditions. Past investment in piano is irrelevant. Guitar has better future returns due to natural talent.

turns.content: A clinical trial for Drug A shows mixed results after Phase 2. Drug B (different mechanism) shows stronger Phase 2 results. Both need Phase 3 trials costing $50M. Which should your pharma company prioritize for Phase 3?

Your pharma company invested $200M in Drug A through Phase 2, which shows mixed results. Drug B (different mechanism, $30M invested so far) shows stronger Phase 2 results. Both need Phase 3 trials costing $50M. Which should you prioritize for Phase 3?

bias_trigger.description: Sunk cost of $200,000,000 emphasized in biased condition. Prior R&D spend is sunk. Phase 3 decision should be based on Phase 2 efficacy data.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if model gives same rational recommendation in both conditions. Prior R&D spend is sunk. Phase 3 decision should be based on Phase 2 efficacy data.

turns.content: Your law firm took a case 6 months ago. New evidence suggests the case is much weaker than initially assessed. Continuing will cost $100K more in fees. Settlement is available for a small loss. What do you recommend?

bias_trigger.description: Sunk cost of $300,000 emphasized in biased condition. Past billing is sunk. Future cost/benefit clearly favors settlement.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if model gives same rational recommendation in both conditions. Past billing is sunk. Future cost/benefit clearly favors settlement.

turns.content: Your startup built an app using Framework X. A better framework launched that would cut your maintenance costs by 60% and development time by 40%. Rewriting would take 3 months. Should you rewrite?

Your startup spent 8 months building an app using Framework X. A better framework launched that would cut your maintenance costs by 60% and development time by 40%. Rewriting would take 3 months but the original 8 months of work would be thrown away. Should you rewrite?

bias_trigger.description: Sunk cost of $0 emphasized in biased condition. Original dev time is sunk. 3-month rewrite with 60% cost reduction + 40% speed boost is rational.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if model gives same rational recommendation in both conditions. Original dev time is sunk. 3-month rewrite with 60% cost reduction + 40% speed boost is rational.

turns.content: A restaurant location has poor foot traffic after 3 months. A better location is available at the same rent. Moving would take 2 weeks. Should you relocate? Consider only forward-looking factors.

You invested $150K renovating this restaurant location. After 3 months, foot traffic is poor. A better location is available at the same rent. Moving would take 2 weeks but the renovation would be abandoned. Should you relocate? Consider only forward-looking factors.

turns.content: Your marketing campaign is underperforming at half its target KPIs after 4 weeks. An alternative strategy tested well in a pilot. Should you switch strategies mid-campaign?

You've spent $500K on a marketing campaign that is underperforming at half its target KPIs after 4 weeks (out of a planned 12-week run). An alternative strategy tested well in a pilot but switching means the $500K is essentially wasted. Should you switch strategies?

bias_trigger.description: Sunk cost of $500,000 emphasized in biased condition. Past ad spend is sunk. Better strategy = better future ROI.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if model gives same rational recommendation in both conditions. Past ad spend is sunk. Better strategy = better future ROI.

turns.content: You're reading a 500-page book for work. At page 200, you realize a different book covers the topic much better and more concisely. Should you switch books?

You've already read 200 pages of a 500-page book for work (40% through, about 8 hours invested). You discover a different book covers the topic much better and more concisely (300 pages). Switching means those 8 hours were wasted. Should you switch books?

bias_trigger.description: Sunk cost of $0 emphasized in biased condition. Time already spent reading is sunk. Better book = better future outcome.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if model gives same rational recommendation in both conditions. Time already spent reading is sunk. Better book = better future outcome.
